entry.title: good things come in threes
entry.summary:
Author: elysianprince

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 10,217Chapters: 2/17Kudos: 36

Summary: "So, what do you say? Just you and me on this one. Like old times?" There was a glimmer of hope in those inhumanly blue eyes of Kent's as he waited for Batman to answer. It was ridiculous to refer to something that was less than a year ago as 'old times,' but Superman had been frustratingly overly-familiar the last few times they had spoken, and it seemed that wasn't something distance had changed. Bruce decided to humor him.Bruce is trying to find the balance between his responsibility for Dick and his partnership with Robin. Clark is trying to navigate catching feelings at the office again, so to speak. Dick just wants to be taken seriously, and maybe for Batman and Superman to stop being so stupid about each other. Somehow, the crime ring trafficking alien creatures in their two cities might just be the answer they've all been looking for.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: Bruce and Clark
entry.summary:
Author: TS_Rhodes

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 2,642Chapters: 2/?Kudos: 15

Summary: When ten-year-old Bruce Wayne's ride breaks down near the Kent farm, he meets ten-year-old Clark, and a friendship forms. The Kents are concerned about Bruce, since he is recently orphaned. Clark, whose powers are just starting to come in, pleads to keep Bruce as a friend. So they invite Bruce and his guardian Alfred to come back and stay the summer. And so it begins...

  • No Archive Warnings Apply
  • Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne
  • Childhood Friends

entry.title: Brightest Star in the Night
entry.summary:
Author: FPeanutButter98

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 16,705Chapters: 6/?Kudos: 131

Summary: When Batman saves an Alien from Luthor, he doesn't expect to fall in love with him. For Kal, Bruce is everything he could have dreamed of. In a world where everything is a secret, having someone by your side is the best superpower of all._Note, you do not need to have read Absolute Superman to understand this fic!

entry.title: How Long Has This Been Going On?
entry.summary:
Author: bigmouthstrikes_again

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 33,380Chapters: 9/12Kudos: 159

Summary: “On the roof. I know what it was,” The boy said.“What was it? What did you see?” Clark said, glancing around.“It was Death I think?” He replied.“Death? What does that mean?”“Death was watching,” John began matter of factly, raising a hand to point behind Clark, “Death was watching and he was looking at you.”Quickly, Clark whipped his head to look in the direction John was pointing. He turned just quickly enough to see a wisp of black disappear behind a building.ORBatman stalks the hell out of Superman. Bruce Wayne is intrigued by Clark Kent. Superman is horrified by Batman. Bumbling reporter Clark Kent can’t seem to figure Bruce Wayne out. Identity mishaps occur.Idea from @lescrenti ‘s TikTok!New chapter every other day sorta :/

entry.title: Coming Home to You
entry.summary:
Author: RandomFan001

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 23,272Chapters: 10/?Kudos: 180

Summary: Clark had long given up his dream of finding his soulmate. The weird feeling he had when his eyes met Batman’s wasn’t anything but nerves and attraction. Definitely not. But when his instincts start acting up, he starts to suspect it’s something else. Worst of all, so does Batman. Featuring; healthy co-parenting, a healthy amount of pining, and a story about finding love and family

entry.title: Spiders and Bats Come From the Dark
entry.summary:
Author: Zoveezz

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 22,772Chapters: 8/?Kudos: 306

Summary: Two years after being erased by the spell, twenty-one-year-old Peter Parker is pulled out of nowhere into another universe, Gotham. Now he has to find a way back to his own, no matter the cost. After a while, he thinks that maybe with the help of these bats he can get back home. ¿Why does he keep feeling like he's already there? ¿And why does Jason keep showing up at his mechanic job on his broken motorcycle?   ------- At twenty, Jason became Red Hood; now, at twenty-two, he still is. He no longer gets along badly with his stupid vigilante family, but he's not comfortable neither. Maybe because that feeling of loneliness, of isolation, is always there, with the rage. He's stressed out with a difficult case when he meets Peter, whom, for some reason, Jason can't stop staring at. And then there was that idiot Spider-Man who was hanging around in his neighborhood.

entry.title: You can take the boy from the circus
entry.summary:
Author: Gotham_Blight

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 93,504Chapters: 20/21Kudos: 611

Summary: Damian Al-Ghul Wayne had everything at one point. He was a prince. Ibn al Xu'ffasch. Son of the bat. Heir to the demon. He had never asked to be ripped away from everything that he knew, from the warmth of his home, his culture, his palace in Nanda Parbat. He had never asked to be shipped to a gloomy, grey city. Even when his father kept taking in orphans and ruining his plans for inheritance, Damian stood steadfast. But then his father died, leaving behind his newest orphan. A tiny acrobat with watery blue eyes and black curls, who spoke no English. Damian knew the boy never asked to be ripped away from everything that he knew, from the warmth of his home, his culture, his circus troupe. Damian knew he needed to be there for him.

entry.title: The Man of Stone of Gotham
entry.summary:
Author: WriterDMathis

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 27,685Chapters: 6/?Kudos: 38

Summary: Sequel to Luminous Fragments From The Veil Above. After the fall of his homeworld, Kal—El begins his new life on Earth, adopted by Bruce Wayne and his four sons. As he discovers Earth, its strange customs, and the warmth of a new family, he also finds love with Bruce—and a home he never thought he’d have. And his Earth name is known as Clark. Clark Wayne.

entry.title: Being part of the Kent-Wayne family isn’t so bad
entry.summary:
Author: PurpleButterfly19

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 18,818Chapters: 5/39Kudos: 74

Summary: It’s been three years since Dick and Kori got married, and many things have changed for the Kent-Wayne family. The family grew larger through more adoptions and also biological ties. New soulmates were discovered, more weddings were held, the Justice League can finally put a name to the man behind the cowl, and Wayne Manor will never look the same again. But the greatest change? Bruce Wayne and Clark Kent are officially engaged. This is the story of how they got there. As for Conner? He couldn’t be doing any better!

entry.title: Two Months In Kansas
entry.summary:
Author: Superbat_fan02

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 5,578Chapters: 3/?Kudos: 22

Summary: Alfred forces Bruce to spend two months on vacation in Kansas, a small, sparsely populated town, to give him a break from his grueling training and the mental exhaustion of spending almost every night at his computer reading thousands of Gotham police reports. On this short trip, Bruce meets Clark, a boy a year younger than him who is his complete opposite. Clark is cheerful, honest, empathetic, and responsible. The golden boy of Kansas, always with a smile and a pleasant tone of voice. But Bruce doesn’t believe that’s the whole story. Clark lives on and grew up on the farm where there are reports of a UFO crash, and, mysteriously, his “parents”—who weren’t parents at the time—are now his parents. Was it really a coincidence that Clark appeared after this supposed UFO news? Furthermore, not to mention that Bruce was already considered tall and well-built for his age, Clark was even taller and bigger than him. He always seemed to hear more than he intended to, or would carelessly lift objects that would be a challenge even for adults to lift. Bruce was sure of it—there was something about Clark, and he wanted to find out what it was.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: Other Things We Haven’t Named
entry.summary:
Author: Anonymous

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 16,473Chapters: 8/10Kudos: 55

Summary: After hundreds of Sunday dinners, Clark is no longer just a welcome presence at Wayne Manor—he’s woven into its routine. As Dick grows older and the house settles into new rhythms, Bruce begins to confront how much has quietly changed. A simple dinner preparation stirs questions about belonging, trust, and what it means to stay. In the warmth of the kitchen, something unspoken edges closer to being named.
